coaching.coaching: Pin down the Project-extensible paragraph update contract with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: the Project-extensible paragraph update contract

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `applies_when` to the move format description in the existing **Project-extensible:** paragraph (or explicitly state in the plan that the paragraph must be updated alongside the new subsection) so the schema description is consistent with the project-portability example.
evidence: Existing doc paragraph: 'a project adds or overrides moves by id via `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` (`{move_id: {name, template}}`' — applies_when is not mentioned.
Plan Approach: 'the exact `project-portability` move id, name, `applies_when`, plus locked template from `truthful-specified-silverfish`' — the subsection will show applies_when.
coach.py MOVE_REGISTRY_SCHEMA confirms applies_when is a first-class schema field: 'applies_when: {type: array, items: {type: string}}'.
validate_move_registry in coach.py line 83 normalizes applies_when as a supported field.
The plan's Approach does not mention updating the Project-extensible paragraph to mention applies_when.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the new subsection to display the project-portability move with `applies_when: [project.portability]`, but never instructs updating the adjacent existing **Project-extensible:** paragraph, which currently describes move format as `{move_id: {name, template}}` — omitting `applies_when`. A reader encountering the paragraph before the subsection will see a schema description that contradicts the example immediately below it.
scenarios: A client reading docs/plan-review-gate.md to author their own project move sees `{move_id: {name, template}}` as the complete format, omits applies_when from their move, and the move fires for all criteria instead of just the intended one — a silent behavioral bug.
The structural documentation test verifies the new subsection's content but does not verify the adjacent paragraph, so the inconsistency goes undetected by test suite.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the existing paragraph to read: `{move_id: {name, template, applies_when?}}` (with a note that `applies_when` scopes the move to specific criterion triggers; absent or empty = always applicable), mirroring the MOVE_REGISTRY_SCHEMA description in coach.py.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Sharpen the `parser_safe_placement` AC to anchor the lower-bound check to a byte offset AFTER the `**Project-extensible:**` paragraph (e.g., after the last occurrence of `{subject}` placeholder text or after the paragraph ending with 'never hand over a solution') rather than 'after the built-in move table header', so the test actually guards the precise insertion location described in the Approach.
evidence: AC: 'The project subsection begins after the built-in move table and ends before `### The advisory cap`'
Approach: 'Add `### Dogfooding a project portability guard` immediately after the existing `**Project-extensible:**` paragraph in `docs/plan-review-gate.md` and before `### The advisory cap`'
doc lines 167–197: `### The Pass-4 move registry` heading at 167, table body at 174–189, `**Project-extensible:**` paragraph at 191–195, `### The advisory cap` at 197
'after the built-in move table header' could anchor to line 167 (heading) or line 174 (table row), both weaker than 'after line 195'
findings.finding: The `parser_safe_placement` AC says 'byte range begins after the built-in move table header,' but the Approach specifies insertion immediately after the `**Project-extensible:**` paragraph — which sits after the full move-table body AND the Project-extensible prose, not merely after the `### The Pass-4 move registry` heading. 'Move table header' is ambiguous between the `###` section heading (line 167) and the actual table header row (line 174); either interpretation yields a weaker lower-bound than the Approach's stated location (end of the `**Project-extensible:**` paragraph at line 195). A test anchored to 'after the section heading' would pass even if the subsection were inserted inside the table itself.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `parser_safe_placement` AC; Approach section paragraph 1
scenarios: A test that checks `subsection_start > doc.index('### The Pass-4 move registry')` would pass even if the subsection is accidentally placed inside the move-table body, defeating the placement guard.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'The project subsection begins after the built-in move table' in the AC with: 'The project subsection begins after the `**Project-extensible:**` paragraph (i.e., after the byte offset of the text `never hand over a solution.`)  and ends before `### The advisory cap`.' Mirror this exact anchor string in the structural test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the exact release threshold values inline in the Approach (recall 1.0, false_accept 0.0, agreement 1.0, per-case stability ≥ 0.6667, Cohen kappa ≥ 0.70) so the subsection content and the calibration_example test assertions are fully specified without requiring a reader to consult `indigoid-unmystic-serpent`.
evidence: Plan Approach only says 'release thresholds from `indigoid-unmystic-serpent`' without enumerating them.
indigoid-unmystic-serpent Approach specifies: 'recall 1.0, false-accept rate 0.0, agreement: 1.0, per-case stability >= 0.6666666667, and Cohen kappa >= 0.70'.

The corresponding AC is 'The subsection contains the eight-case calibration path plus its release thresholds. Prove with ... -k calibration_example' — the test must assert exact literals, but the plan does not state which literals.
The structural test (test_project_portability_docs.py -k calibration_example) will need specific string literals to search for, yet the plan does not specify which strings to assert.
findings.finding: The plan's Approach says the subsection will contain 'the balanced eight-case eval path plus release thresholds from `indigoid-unmystic-serpent`' but does not enumerate the metric names or threshold values inline. An executor writing the subsection must read the dependent child ticket to know exactly which metrics (recall, false_accept, agreement, kappa, stability) and which exact values (1.0, 0.0, 1.0, ≥0.6667, ≥0.70) to document — the processing protocol is not co-located with the plan that requires it.
findings.location: Approach section — 'the balanced eight-case calibration path plus release thresholds from `indigoid-unmystic-serpent`'
scenarios: An executor writes the subsection with approximate or paraphrased thresholds; the calibration_example test either passes loosely or fails because the exact expected strings are not specified in either the plan or the test spec.
An implementer omits the kappa threshold from the documentation because it is mentioned only in the child ticket's Approach prose, which they may not re-read when authoring the doc.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Approach: 'The release thresholds are: recall = 1.0, false_accept = 0.0, agreement = 1.0, per-case stability ≥ 0.6666666667, Cohen kappa ≥ 0.70 (fire/no-fire agreement over eight cases, not judge alignment).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
